python.title: Python Syntax and Basics
python.description: Learn Python syntax, data types, control flow, and how to set up a virtual environment with pip. This layer establishes the foundation for writing readable, idiomatic Python code.

resources.label: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python

python.challenge: Build a command-line number guessing game that tracks attempts and uses a virtual environment with a requirements.txt file.

python.title: Functions, Modules, and Packages
python.description: Master Python functions including default arguments, *args, **kwargs, and lambda expressions. Learn to organize code into modules and packages and import them correctly.
python.challenge: Extend the guessing game into a multi-module package with a utils module for input validation and a stats module for tracking scores.

python.title: Object-Oriented Python
python.description: Understand Python classes, inheritance, dunder methods, and properties to model real-world problems. Learn how Python OOP differs from other languages through special method protocols.
python.challenge: Refactor the guessing game into an OOP design with a Game class, a Player class, and a Leaderboard class using dunder methods for comparison.

python.title: Iterators, Generators, and Comprehensions
python.description: Dive into Python's iteration protocol, generator functions, and the expressive power of list and dict comprehensions. These tools are central to idiomatic, memory-efficient Python.
python.challenge: Add a streaming stats feature to the game that uses a generator to lazily compute running averages and percentiles across sessions.

python.title: Error Handling, Context Managers, and File I/O
python.description: Learn robust exception handling, the with statement, and reading and writing files including JSON and CSV. These patterns are essential for reliable, production-quality Python programs.
python.challenge: Add a persistent leaderboard to the game that saves and loads scores from a JSON file using a custom context manager for safe file access.

python.title: Decorators, Closures, and Functional Patterns
python.description: Understand closures and how decorators work under the hood to add behavior to functions without modifying them. Learn functools and common functional programming patterns in Python.
python.challenge: Add a @retry decorator and a @timer decorator to the game engine, and use lru_cache for caching difficulty level configurations.

python.title: Testing with pytest
python.description: Write unit and integration tests using pytest, fixtures, and parametrize to ensure code correctness. Learn test-driven development workflows and how to measure code coverage.
python.challenge: Write a full pytest suite for the game project covering all modules, using fixtures for player setup, mocking for file I/O, and achieving 90%+ coverage.

python.title: Concurrency - Threads, Async, and Multiprocessing
python.description: Learn the difference between threading, multiprocessing, and asyncio, and when to use each for I/O-bound versus CPU-bound tasks. Master async/await syntax for modern asynchronous Python.

resources.label: Python Concurrency from the Ground Up

python.challenge: Add an async leaderboard API client to the game that fetches global high scores concurrently using asyncio and aiohttp.

python.title: Type Hints, Protocols, and Modern Python Idioms
python.description: Apply full type annotations, use mypy for static analysis, and learn structural subtyping via Protocols. Explore modern Python idioms introduced in 3.10+ including pattern matching.
python.challenge: Fully annotate the game project, pass mypy --strict with zero errors, and refactor game state handling to use pattern matching.

python.title: Production Python - Packaging, Performance, and Ecosystem
python.description: Learn to package and publish Python projects, profile and optimize performance, and navigate the broader Python ecosystem for production use. Understand CI/CD integration and dependency management best practices.
topics: Dependency management with pip-tools or poetry
CI/CD with GitHub Actions for Python
python.challenge: Package the completed game project as a distributable CLI tool on PyPI with a pyproject.toml, GitHub Actions CI running pytest, and a profiled, optimized game loop.

javascript.title: JavaScript Syntax and Fundamentals
javascript.description: Learn the core syntax of JavaScript including variables, types, operators, and control flow in both browser and Node.js environments. Understand the dynamic and loosely-typed nature of the language.
topics: Running JS in browser and Node.js
javascript.challenge: Build a command-line to-do list app in Node.js that supports add, list, and remove commands using only core JS and no external libraries.

javascript.title: Functions, Closures, and Scope
javascript.description: Deeply understand JavaScript function mechanics including closures, the call stack, and how scope chains work. These concepts are the bedrock of advanced JavaScript patterns.

resources.label: You Don't Know JS - Scope and Closures

javascript.challenge: Add a module pattern to the to-do app using closures to encapsulate state, with a factory function that creates isolated to-do lists.

javascript.title: Objects, Prototypes, and Classes
javascript.description: Understand JavaScript's prototype chain, object creation patterns, and how ES6 classes are syntactic sugar over prototypal inheritance. Learn modern class features including private fields and static methods.

resources.label: You Don't Know JS - this and Object Prototypes

javascript.challenge: Refactor the to-do app into a class-based design with a TodoList class using private fields for the items array and static factory methods.

javascript.title: Asynchronous JavaScript
javascript.description: Master the asynchronous programming model in JavaScript using callbacks, Promises, and async/await. Understand the event loop and how JavaScript handles concurrency in a single-threaded environment.

resources.label: You Don't Know JS - Async and Performance

javascript.challenge: Add async file persistence to the to-do app using Node.js fs/promises, with error handling for missing files and concurrent read/write safety.

javascript.title: DOM Manipulation and Browser APIs
javascript.description: Learn to interact with the browser DOM, handle events, and use Web APIs including fetch, localStorage, and the History API. Build dynamic, interactive web pages without a framework.
javascript.challenge: Build a browser-based UI for the to-do app using vanilla JS and the DOM, persisting data to localStorage and supporting drag-to-reorder.

javascript.title: Modern ES2022+ Features
javascript.description: Explore modern JavaScript features from ES2015 through ES2022+ including destructuring, spread, optional chaining, and top-level await. Staying current with the language makes code more expressive and concise.
javascript.challenge: Refactor the entire to-do app codebase to use modern syntax throughout - destructuring, optional chaining, and logical assignment where appropriate.

javascript.title: Modules, Tooling, and Node.js Ecosystem
javascript.description: Learn ES Modules and CommonJS, understand the Node.js module resolution algorithm, and get comfortable with the npm ecosystem and basic build tooling. These skills bridge browser and server-side JavaScript.
javascript.challenge: Convert the to-do app to use ES Modules throughout, add an npm script for bundling with Vite, and extract shared utilities into a standalone npm-style module.

javascript.title: Error Handling, Debugging, and Testing
javascript.description: Write robust error handling strategies, learn to use browser and Node.js debuggers effectively, and write tests using a modern JavaScript testing framework. Reliable code requires intentional error management and test coverage.
topics: try/catch/finally in sync and async code
javascript.challenge: Write a Vitest suite covering all to-do app logic, mock localStorage in tests, and add a global error boundary that logs uncaught errors to a logfile.

javascript.title: Functional Programming Patterns in JavaScript
javascript.description: Apply functional programming principles to JavaScript including immutability, pure functions, currying, and function composition. These patterns lead to more predictable, testable code.

resources.label: Mostly Adequate Guide to Functional Programming

javascript.challenge: Refactor the to-do app state management using pure functions and immutable updates, composing transformation pipelines for filtering and sorting tasks.

javascript.title: Production JavaScript - Performance, Security, and Ecosystem
javascript.description: Learn performance profiling, memory leak detection, security best practices like XSS prevention, and how to navigate the production JavaScript ecosystem. Ship code that is fast, secure, and maintainable at scale.
javascript.challenge: Audit the completed to-do app for XSS vulnerabilities, add a Content Security Policy, profile and optimize the render loop, and add a Web Worker for bulk task processing.

typescript.title: TypeScript Setup and Basic Types
typescript.description: Install TypeScript, configure tsconfig.json, and learn the basic type system including primitives, arrays, and union types. Understand how TypeScript compiles to JavaScript and how to run TS in Node.js.
typescript.challenge: Build a typed command-line inventory tracker in TypeScript with strict mode enabled, using interfaces to define item shapes.

typescript.title: Interfaces, Type Aliases, and Enums
typescript.description: Learn to model data using interfaces and type aliases, understand when to use each, and use enums for fixed sets of named values. These constructs are the backbone of TypeScript domain modeling.
typescript.challenge: Model the inventory tracker domain with interfaces for Item, Category, and Warehouse, using string enums for item status and readonly for immutable fields.

typescript.title: Functions and Type Narrowing
typescript.description: Type function signatures precisely including overloads and return types, and use type narrowing to safely handle union types at runtime. TypeScript's control-flow analysis makes these patterns highly ergonomic.
topics: Typed function parameters and return types
typescript.challenge: Add typed search and filter functions to the inventory tracker using discriminated unions for search result types and type predicates for item validation.

typescript.title: Generics
typescript.description: Write reusable, type-safe code using generics in functions, interfaces, and classes. Generics are the most powerful feature of TypeScript's type system for building flexible abstractions.
typescript.challenge: Create a generic Repository<T> class for the inventory tracker that handles CRUD operations with full type safety for any entity type.

typescript.title: Classes and Decorators
typescript.description: Use TypeScript classes with access modifiers, abstract classes, and decorators to build structured, extensible code. Decorators enable metadata-driven patterns common in frameworks like NestJS and Angular.
typescript.challenge: Refactor the inventory tracker using abstract base classes and add a @log decorator that records all repository method calls with timestamps.

typescript.title: Advanced Types - Mapped, Conditional, and Template Literal
typescript.description: Master TypeScript's advanced type-level programming including mapped types, conditional types, and template literal types. These features let you express complex type transformations that eliminate entire categories of bugs.
topics: Conditional types (T extends U ? X : Y)
typescript.challenge: Build utility types for the inventory tracker: a DeepReadonly<T> type, a PickByValue<T, V> helper, and a typed event emitter using template literal types.

typescript.title: Modules, Declaration Files, and tsconfig Mastery
typescript.description: Understand TypeScript module resolution, write declaration files for untyped libraries, and configure tsconfig.json for different build targets and project references. Proper project configuration is critical for large TypeScript codebases.
typescript.challenge: Split the inventory tracker into a multi-package monorepo using TypeScript project references, with separate packages for core types, repository, and CLI.

typescript.title: Async TypeScript and Error Handling Patterns
typescript.description: Write async/await code with precise typed error handling, use Result types for explicit error paths, and type complex async workflows. TypeScript makes asynchronous code safer when types are applied correctly.
typescript.challenge: Add an async export feature to the inventory tracker using a Result<T, E> type for error handling, with typed retry logic and cancellation support.

typescript.title: Testing TypeScript Code
typescript.description: Write type-safe tests using Vitest or Jest with TypeScript, test type correctness with tsd or expect-type, and use advanced mocking with typed stubs. Testing TypeScript code requires understanding both runtime and compile-time correctness.
typescript.challenge: Write a comprehensive Vitest suite for the inventory tracker including type tests for all generic utilities, mocked repository tests, and a CI workflow on GitHub Actions.

typescript.title: Production TypeScript - Build Pipelines, Performance, and Ecosystem
typescript.description: Ship production TypeScript with optimized build pipelines, understand compiler performance tuning, and navigate the TypeScript ecosystem including popular frameworks and toolchains. Mature TypeScript projects require intentional configuration and build strategy.
topics: esbuild and swc for fast builds
typescript.challenge: Publish the inventory tracker core package to a local npm registry with proper declaration files, an optimized esbuild pipeline, and a migration guide doc for JS-to-TS onboarding.

go.title: Go Syntax and Toolchain
go.description: Set up the Go toolchain, understand the module system, and learn core syntax including variables, types, loops, and functions. Go's simplicity is intentional - the language is small and easy to hold in your head.
topics: go mod init and module structure
go.challenge: Build a command-line URL shortener in Go that stores mappings in memory and supports add, lookup, and list commands.

go.title: Arrays, Slices, Maps, and Structs
go.description: Master Go's composite types - slices, maps, and structs - and understand the memory model behind them including capacity and append behavior. These types are the foundation of all Go data modeling.

resources.label: Go by Example - Structs and Maps

go.challenge: Refactor the URL shortener to use a struct for the store, add metadata fields (created_at, hit_count) and implement a slice-based history log.

go.title: Interfaces and Methods
go.description: Learn Go's implicit interface satisfaction model, method sets, and how to use interfaces for polymorphism and testability. Go interfaces are small and composable - the standard library demonstrates this with io.Reader and io.Writer.

resources.label: The Go Programming Language - Interfaces Chapter

go.challenge: Refactor the URL shortener storage behind a Store interface, implement an in-memory and a file-backed version, and swap them at startup via a flag.

go.title: Error Handling
go.description: Embrace Go's explicit error handling model, define custom error types, and learn idiomatic patterns like wrapping and sentinel errors. Go's approach to errors is deliberate - errors are values, not exceptions.
go.challenge: Add comprehensive error types to the URL shortener (NotFoundError, DuplicateError, ValidationError) with full wrapping, sentinel checks, and user-friendly messages.

go.title: Goroutines and Channels
go.description: Harness Go's concurrency primitives - goroutines and channels - to write concurrent programs that are safe and expressive. Go's CSP model encourages sharing memory by communicating rather than communicating by sharing memory.
go.challenge: Add a concurrent analytics pipeline to the URL shortener that processes click events through a channel and aggregates stats in a background goroutine.

go.title: The Standard Library
go.description: Explore Go's rich standard library covering HTTP, JSON, file I/O, time, and strings. Go's stdlib is deliberately comprehensive so you can build production-quality programs with minimal external dependencies.
go.challenge: Expose the URL shortener as an HTTP API using only net/http, with JSON request/response bodies, proper status codes, and structured logging.

go.title: Testing in Go
go.description: Write table-driven tests, benchmarks, and examples using Go's built-in testing package, and use interfaces for testability. Go's testing tooling is built into the language - no external framework is needed.
go.challenge: Write a full table-driven test suite for the URL shortener including HTTP handler tests using httptest, benchmarks for the store, and coverage above 85%.

go.title: Go Modules and Dependency Management
go.description: Manage Go module dependencies with go.mod and go.sum, understand versioning semantics, and use popular third-party packages responsibly. The Go module system provides reproducible builds by design.
topics: go get, go tidy, go vendor
go.challenge: Add a third-party router (chi or gorilla/mux) and a structured logger (zerolog) to the URL shortener, managing versions with go.mod and tidying dependencies.

go.title: Advanced Go Patterns - Generics, Context, and sync
go.description: Use Go generics (added in 1.18) for reusable data structures, propagate cancellation with context.Context, and synchronize goroutines safely with the sync package. These are the tools of idiomatic production Go.
go.challenge: Add a generic cache layer to the URL shortener with configurable TTL using context for cancellation, and protect concurrent access with sync.RWMutex.

go.title: Production Go - Build, Deploy, and Ecosystem
go.description: Build production Go binaries with cross-compilation, profiling, and embed, and understand the broader Go ecosystem for web, CLI, and systems programming. Go's single-binary deployment model is one of its greatest production advantages.
topics: Go ecosystem overview (Gin, Echo, GORM, Cobra)

resources.label: 100 Go Mistakes and How to Avoid Them

go.challenge: Package the URL shortener as a cross-compiled binary with embedded static assets, a Dockerfile for deployment, graceful shutdown, and pprof endpoints for profiling.

rust.title: Rust Setup and Basic Syntax
rust.description: Install Rust via rustup, understand Cargo as the build system and package manager, and learn Rust basic syntax. Rust's compiler is unusually helpful - learning to read its error messages is a core skill.
rust.challenge: Build a Cargo project with a CLI task manager that can add and list tasks, reading compiler errors carefully to understand Rust's rules from the start.

rust.title: Ownership, Borrowing, and Lifetimes
rust.description: Internalize Rust's ownership system - the most unique and important feature of the language - including moves, borrows, and lifetime annotations. Ownership is what makes Rust memory-safe without a garbage collector.
rust.challenge: Refactor the task manager to pass tasks by reference through helper functions, adding lifetime annotations where necessary and eliminating all unnecessary clones.

rust.title: Structs, Enums, and Pattern Matching
rust.description: Model domains using Rust structs and enums, and use pattern matching to exhaustively handle variants. Rust enums are algebraic data types - far more powerful than enums in most languages.

resources.label: The Rust Book - Enums and Pattern Matching

rust.challenge: Refactor the task manager to use a Task enum with variants (Todo, InProgress, Done) each carrying relevant data, and use match everywhere for status transitions.

rust.title: Collections, Iterators, and Closures
rust.description: Use Vec, HashMap, and HashSet from the standard library, and master Rust's powerful iterator adapters combined with closures. Rust iterators are lazy and zero-cost - using them is idiomatic and efficient.
topics: Iterator trait and adapters (map, filter, fold)
rust.challenge: Add filtering, sorting, and grouping features to the task manager using iterator adapters, storing tasks in a HashMap keyed by project name.

rust.title: Traits and Generics
rust.description: Define and implement traits to share behavior across types, and write generic functions and structs constrained by trait bounds. Rust traits are the foundation of zero-cost abstraction.
rust.challenge: Add a generic Storage<T> trait to the task manager with in-memory and file-backed implementations, and a Display impl for pretty-printing all task types.

rust.title: Error Handling with Result and the ? Operator
rust.description: Write idiomatic Rust error handling using Result<T, E>, the ? operator for propagation, and custom error types. The thiserror and anyhow crates make production error handling ergonomic.
rust.challenge: Add comprehensive error handling to the task manager using thiserror for a library error type and anyhow in main, propagating all I/O errors with context.

rust.title: Modules, Crates, and Cargo Deep Dive
rust.description: Organize Rust code into modules and crates, manage visibility, and use Cargo workspaces for multi-crate projects. Understanding the module system is essential for building maintainable Rust projects.
rust.challenge: Restructure the task manager as a Cargo workspace with a core library crate and a CLI crate, exposing a clean public API from the library.

rust.title: Lifetimes Advanced and Smart Pointers
rust.description: Go beyond basic lifetimes into advanced scenarios involving structs with references, and learn smart pointers (Box, Rc, RefCell, Arc) for heap allocation and shared ownership. These tools unlock patterns that simple borrowing cannot express.
rust.challenge: Add a shared configuration system to the task manager using Arc<RwLock<Config>> so multiple components can read config concurrently with safe mutation.

rust.title: Concurrency and Async Rust
rust.description: Use Rust threads and message-passing channels for parallelism, and learn async/await with the Tokio runtime for high-performance I/O. Rust guarantees thread safety at compile time - the compiler prevents data races.
topics: Mutex<T> and Arc for shared state
rust.challenge: Add an async sync feature to the task manager using Tokio that fetches remote task updates from an HTTP endpoint concurrently with local operations.

rust.title: Production Rust - Testing, Performance, and Ecosystem
rust.description: Write unit and integration tests in Rust, benchmark with Criterion, profile binaries, and survey the production Rust ecosystem for web, CLI, and systems use cases. Rust in production rewards investment in tooling and ecosystem knowledge.
rust.challenge: Finalize the task manager with a full test suite, Criterion benchmarks for storage operations, a Clap-powered CLI, and Serde JSON serialization for data persistence.

java.title: Java Syntax and JVM Basics
java.description: Set up the JDK, understand the Java compilation model and JVM, and learn core syntax including variables, control flow, and methods. Java's verbosity is intentional - readability at scale is a core design goal.
java.challenge: Build a command-line bank account simulator in Java with deposit, withdraw, and balance commands as a Maven or Gradle project.

java.title: Object-Oriented Programming in Java
java.description: Master Java OOP fundamentals - classes, inheritance, polymorphism, and encapsulation - which are the bedrock of the entire Java ecosystem. Every Java library and framework is built on these principles.
java.challenge: Refactor the bank simulator into an OOP design with Account, SavingsAccount, and CheckingAccount classes using inheritance and polymorphic transaction handling.

java.title: Collections Framework and Generics
java.description: Use the Java Collections Framework effectively - List, Set, Map, and Queue - and write generic classes and methods for type-safe reusable code. The collections framework is one of Java's most-used APIs.
java.challenge: Add a transaction history using a LinkedList and a sorted index using TreeMap to the bank simulator, with generic utility methods for balance calculations.

java.title: Exception Handling and File I/O
java.description: Handle exceptions correctly in Java using checked and unchecked exception hierarchies, and read and write files using the NIO.2 API. Java's explicit exception handling forces you to think about failure modes.
topics: Reading and writing text and binary files
java.challenge: Add persistent account storage to the bank simulator using java.nio.file and JSON serialization, with custom exceptions for insufficient funds and invalid accounts.

java.title: Streams API and Lambdas
java.description: Transform, filter, and aggregate data using the Java Streams API and lambda expressions introduced in Java 8. Streams are the idiomatic way to process collections in modern Java and often replace explicit loops.
java.challenge: Rewrite all account querying and reporting logic in the bank simulator using Streams, grouping transactions by type using Collectors.groupingBy.

java.title: Concurrency and Multithreading
java.description: Write concurrent Java programs using threads, the Executor framework, and concurrent collections. Java has one of the most mature concurrency APIs in any language, built into the standard library.
java.challenge: Add concurrent transaction processing to the bank simulator using a thread pool, protecting balance state with appropriate synchronization primitives.

java.title: Design Patterns in Java
java.description: Apply classic Gang of Four design patterns in Java - singleton, builder, factory, observer, and strategy - and understand when each applies. Design patterns are the shared vocabulary of Java software architecture.
java.challenge: Refactor the bank simulator using the Builder pattern for account creation, Strategy for transaction fee calculation, and Observer for balance change notifications.

java.title: Modern Java - Records, Sealed Classes, and Pattern Matching
java.description: Adopt modern Java features from Java 14-21 including records, sealed classes, text blocks, and pattern matching for switch. Modern Java is significantly more expressive than the Java 8 many developers learned.
java.challenge: Refactor transaction types in the bank simulator to sealed interfaces with record implementations, using pattern matching switch for all transaction dispatch logic.

java.title: Testing with JUnit 5 and Mockito
java.description: Write unit and integration tests using JUnit 5 with parameterized tests and nested test classes, and mock dependencies with Mockito. Testing is a first-class practice in Java - the ecosystem is among the best in any language.
java.challenge: Write a complete JUnit 5 test suite for the bank simulator with Mockito mocks for external storage, parameterized tests for edge cases, and JaCoCo coverage above 80%.

java.title: Production Java - Build Tools, Performance, and Ecosystem
java.description: Ship production Java with Maven or Gradle, profile JVM performance with JFR and JMC, and navigate the Java ecosystem including Spring, Micronaut, and Jakarta EE. Java's mature ecosystem means there is a well-tested library for nearly every problem.
java.challenge: Package the bank simulator as a fat JAR with Maven, profile it with JFR under simulated load, tune the GC settings, and add a Spring Boot HTTP layer as a bonus extension.

cpp.title: C++ Syntax and Compilation Model
cpp.description: Set up a C++ compiler (GCC or Clang), understand the preprocessing, compilation, and linking pipeline, and learn basic syntax. C++ is a compiled systems language where understanding the build model is as important as the syntax.
cpp.challenge: Build a command-line contact book in C++ with add, search, and list commands, compiled with CMake and organized into header and source files.

cpp.title: Pointers, References, and Memory Management
cpp.description: Understand pointers and references, manual memory management with new and delete, and why unmanaged memory is dangerous. This layer is where C++ diverges most sharply from other modern languages.
cpp.challenge: Rewrite the contact book using raw pointers for dynamic storage, then intentionally introduce and diagnose a memory leak using Valgrind.

cpp.title: Classes, OOP, and RAII
cpp.description: Define C++ classes with constructors, destructors, and operator overloading, and learn RAII - the pattern of tying resource lifetimes to object lifetimes. RAII is the fundamental C++ idiom for safe resource management.
cpp.challenge: Refactor the contact book into a RAII-safe class design where the ContactBook destructor releases all memory, with copy and move constructors implemented correctly.

cpp.title: STL Containers and Algorithms
cpp.description: Use the C++ Standard Template Library containers (vector, map, set, unordered_map) and algorithms (sort, find, transform) to write efficient, idiomatic C++. The STL is one of the most powerful standard libraries in any language.
cpp.challenge: Replace all raw arrays in the contact book with vector and unordered_map, and use STL algorithms for all searching and sorting operations.

cpp.title: Templates and Generic Programming
cpp.description: Write function templates and class templates to create generic, reusable code that works across types without runtime overhead. C++ templates are Turing-complete and enable zero-cost abstractions unique to the language.
cpp.challenge: Add a generic Cache<K, V> template class to the contact book with configurable eviction policy, specializing it for string keys with case-insensitive lookup.

cpp.title: Smart Pointers and Modern Memory Management
cpp.description: Replace raw pointers with smart pointers (unique_ptr, shared_ptr, weak_ptr) for automatic, safe memory management. Modern C++ code should rarely if ever use new and delete directly.

resources.label: Effective Modern C++ - Smart Pointer Items

cpp.challenge: Refactor the entire contact book to use smart pointers exclusively, eliminating all raw new/delete, and demonstrate correct use of weak_ptr for observer relationships.

cpp.title: Lambdas, Ranges, and Modern C++ Idioms
cpp.description: Use C++11/14/17/20 modern idioms including lambdas, range-based for, structured bindings, and the Ranges library. Modern C++ reads very differently from legacy C++ - knowing the new idioms is essential.
cpp.challenge: Rewrite all contact book filtering and querying using C++20 Ranges with view pipelines, structured bindings for result unpacking, and std::optional for nullable results.

cpp.title: Concurrency in C++
cpp.description: Write multi-threaded C++ programs using std::thread, mutexes, condition variables, and atomics from the C++11 concurrency library. C++ concurrency requires explicit management - the memory model is the foundation.
cpp.challenge: Add a background auto-save thread to the contact book that periodically serializes data, using a mutex-protected queue for pending write operations.

cpp.title: Error Handling and Robust C++
cpp.description: Handle errors in C++ using exceptions, noexcept, std::error_code, and defensive programming techniques. C++ offers multiple error-handling strategies - choosing the right one for each context is a mark of expertise.
cpp.challenge: Add strong exception safety guarantees to all ContactBook mutating operations and use std::error_code for file I/O error reporting without throwing.

cpp.title: Production C++ - Build Systems, Profiling, and Ecosystem
cpp.description: Build production C++ with CMake, profile with perf or Instruments, use sanitizers to catch bugs, and understand the C++ ecosystem including Boost, Conan, and vcpkg. Production C++ requires mastery of the full toolchain.
topics: CMake targets and modern CMake patterns
cpp.challenge: Finalize the contact book with a CMake build, Google Test suite, AddressSanitizer clean run, Conan-managed dependencies, and a perf profile showing no hot spots in critical paths.

csharp.title: C# Syntax and .NET Setup
csharp.description: Set up the .NET SDK, create and run C# projects with the dotnet CLI, and learn basic C# syntax. C# is a modern, statically typed language that runs on the .NET runtime across Windows, Linux, and macOS.
csharp.challenge: Build a command-line expense tracker in C# using the dotnet CLI, with add, list, and total commands.

csharp.title: Object-Oriented C#
csharp.description: Use C# classes, inheritance, interfaces, and properties to build structured object-oriented programs. C# OOP has several features not found in Java - properties, partial classes, and explicit interface implementation among them.
csharp.challenge: Refactor the expense tracker into an OOP design with Expense, Category, and Budget classes using properties and interface-based storage.

csharp.title: Collections, Generics, and LINQ Basics
csharp.description: Use .NET generic collections (List<T>, Dictionary<K,V>, HashSet<T>) and write your first LINQ queries to filter and project data. LINQ is one of C# most distinctive and powerful features.
csharp.challenge: Use LINQ to add reporting features to the expense tracker - top categories, monthly totals, and expenses above a threshold - using method chaining.

csharp.title: Exception Handling and File I/O
csharp.description: Write robust exception handling using C# try/catch/finally, custom exceptions, and the using statement for resource management. Use System.IO for file operations and System.Text.Json for serialization.
csharp.challenge: Add JSON file persistence to the expense tracker using System.Text.Json with custom exceptions for invalid data and a using statement for file handle safety.

csharp.title: Async/Await and the Task Parallel Library
csharp.description: Write asynchronous C# code using async/await and the Task Parallel Library for non-blocking I/O and parallel CPU work. C# async/await is one of the most ergonomic async models in any mainstream language.
csharp.challenge: Add async file I/O and an async HTTP export feature to the expense tracker using async/await throughout with CancellationToken support.

csharp.title: Advanced LINQ and Functional Patterns
csharp.description: Go deeper with LINQ including grouping, joining, and custom operators, and apply functional patterns like immutable records and expression trees. LINQ's composability enables a functional style that integrates naturally with C# OOP.
csharp.challenge: Replace all expense filtering with advanced LINQ pipelines using GroupBy for category reports, switch expressions for expense classification, and immutable record types.

csharp.title: Dependency Injection and Architecture Patterns
csharp.description: Understand dependency injection as built into .NET, and apply common architecture patterns (repository, service layer, options pattern) used throughout the .NET ecosystem. DI is not a framework choice in .NET - it is a core platform feature.
csharp.challenge: Refactor the expense tracker to use the .NET DI container with constructor-injected services, a repository interface, and ILogger for structured logging.

csharp.title: Testing with xUnit and Moq
csharp.description: Write unit and integration tests using xUnit, mock dependencies with Moq, and measure coverage. The .NET testing ecosystem is mature and well-integrated with the dotnet CLI and CI systems.
csharp.challenge: Write an xUnit test suite for the expense tracker using Moq for storage dependencies, Theory tests for edge cases, and FluentAssertions for readable assertions.

csharp.title: Modern C# Features - Nullable Reference Types, Spans, and Channels
csharp.description: Enable nullable reference types to eliminate null reference exceptions at compile time, use Span<T> for allocation-free data processing, and use System.Threading.Channels for async producer-consumer patterns. These features characterize modern, performance-conscious C#.
csharp.challenge: Enable nullable reference types across the expense tracker with zero warnings, add an IAsyncEnumerable export stream, and use Channels for a background notification pipeline.

csharp.title: Production C# - NuGet, Performance, and Ecosystem
csharp.description: Publish NuGet packages, profile .NET apps with dotnet-trace and BenchmarkDotNet, and survey the .NET ecosystem for web, desktop, and cloud development. Production .NET requires understanding the full platform beyond the language itself.
topics: Ecosystem overview (ASP.NET Core, EF Core, MAUI, Blazor)
csharp.challenge: Package the expense tracker core library as a NuGet package, run BenchmarkDotNet benchmarks on LINQ queries, publish results, and add a GitHub Actions workflow with test and publish steps.

ruby.title: Ruby Syntax and the Ruby Way
ruby.description: Install Ruby with rbenv or rvm, learn the fundamental syntax, and begin developing a feel for Ruby's philosophy of developer happiness and expressiveness. Ruby is designed to be read like natural language - idiomatic Ruby embraces this.
ruby.challenge: Build a command-line quiz game in Ruby that reads questions from a YAML file, tracks score, and runs in a tight game loop.

ruby.title: Collections - Arrays, Hashes, and Enumerables
ruby.description: Master Ruby arrays, hashes, and the Enumerable module which provides an incredibly rich set of collection methods. Enumerable is one of the most elegant parts of the Ruby language and is used throughout idiomatic Ruby code.
ruby.challenge: Add leaderboard tracking to the quiz game using hashes, sort and filter results with Enumerable methods, and export scores to a CSV using map.

ruby.title: Blocks, Procs, and Lambdas
ruby.description: Understand Ruby blocks as first-class constructs, the difference between Proc and Lambda, and how to write methods that yield to blocks. Blocks are the foundation of Ruby's DSL capabilities and iterative APIs.
ruby.challenge: Refactor the quiz game to use blocks for question rendering, add a timing feature using yield with a timer block, and create a configurable scoring lambda.

ruby.title: Object-Oriented Ruby
ruby.description: Build Ruby classes with the initialize method, attr_accessor, modules for mixins, and understand Ruby's open class model. Ruby OOP is dynamic and flexible - classes can be reopened and modified at any time.
ruby.challenge: Refactor the quiz game into a full OOP design with Quiz, Question, Player, and Leaderboard classes, using the Comparable mixin for player ranking.

ruby.title: Metaprogramming Fundamentals
ruby.description: Begin exploring Ruby's metaprogramming capabilities - method_missing, define_method, send, and class macros - which enable Ruby's expressive DSLs and frameworks. Metaprogramming is where Ruby becomes truly unique.
ruby.challenge: Add a DSL to the quiz game that allows defining questions declaratively using class macros (e.g., question 'What is...?' do ... end) powered by define_method.

ruby.title: Exception Handling and File I/O
ruby.description: Handle exceptions idiomatically in Ruby using rescue, ensure, retry, and custom exception classes, and read and write files and JSON. Ruby exception handling is concise - many operations have built-in rescue shorthand.
ruby.challenge: Add JSON persistence to the quiz game leaderboard with custom exceptions for file corruption, retry logic for transient I/O errors, and ensure for cleanup.

ruby.title: Gems and Bundler
ruby.description: Create and publish Ruby gems, manage dependencies with Bundler and Gemfile, and understand gem versioning. The Ruby gem ecosystem is vast - learning to navigate it and create gems yourself is essential.
topics: gem dependencies and runtime vs development
ruby.challenge: Package the quiz game as a gem with a Gemfile, gemspec, and a command-line binary, and publish it to a local gem server.

ruby.title: Testing with RSpec
ruby.description: Write expressive, behavior-driven tests using RSpec with describe, context, and it blocks, and mock with RSpec mocks or Mocha. RSpec's DSL makes tests read almost like documentation.
ruby.challenge: Write a complete RSpec suite for the quiz game covering all classes, using doubles for file I/O, shared examples for game rules, and SimpleCov for coverage reporting.

ruby.title: Advanced Metaprogramming and DSL Design
ruby.description: Build internal DSLs using instance_eval, class_eval, and the full suite of Ruby metaprogramming tools. Understanding how Rails, RSpec, and Sinatra build their DSLs will make you a far more capable Ruby developer.
ruby.challenge: Build a configuration DSL for the quiz game using instance_eval so game settings can be expressed as a clean Ruby block without hash syntax.

ruby.title: Production Ruby - Concurrency, Performance, and Ecosystem
ruby.description: Understand Ruby's GIL and concurrency options (threads, Ractors, async gem), profile Ruby code with ruby-prof and stackprof, and survey the production Ruby ecosystem. Ruby's production maturity is demonstrated by decades of Rails applications running at scale.
ruby.challenge: Add concurrent question fetching to the quiz game using Ruby threads with Mutex protection, profile the hot path with stackprof, and document an upgrade path to Ractors.

php.title: PHP Syntax and Basics
php.description: Learn PHP syntax, data types, operators, and control flow structures. Understand how PHP scripts execute in a server context.
php.challenge: Build a PHP script that reads a list of student names and scores from an array, calculates the average, and prints a formatted report.

php.title: Forms, Superglobals, and HTTP
php.description: Handle HTTP requests and user input using PHP superglobals. Learn session and cookie management for stateful web interactions.
php.challenge: Add a registration form to your project that validates input, stores user data in a session, and displays a personalized welcome page.

php.title: Object-Oriented PHP
php.description: Master PHP classes, inheritance, interfaces, and traits. Apply OOP principles to structure reusable and maintainable code.
php.challenge: Refactor your project into classes: a User class, a ReportGenerator class, and an interface for data sources.

php.title: Composer and Dependency Management
php.description: Use Composer to manage packages and autoload classes following PSR-4 conventions. Understand the composer.json lifecycle.
php.challenge: Add Composer to your project, use PSR-4 autoloading for all classes, and integrate one third-party package such as a validator or mailer.

php.title: Databases and PDO
php.description: Connect PHP to relational databases using PDO with prepared statements to prevent SQL injection. Perform CRUD operations safely.
php.challenge: Persist your project data in MySQL via PDO, supporting full CRUD for users and reports with proper prepared statements.

php.title: PSR Standards and Clean Architecture
php.description: Follow PSR-1, PSR-2, PSR-3, and PSR-12 coding standards to write interoperable PHP. Integrate a PSR-3 compliant logger.
php.challenge: Enforce PSR-12 style across your codebase using PHP_CodeSniffer and add structured logging with Monolog for all key actions.

php.title: Testing PHP Applications
php.description: Write unit and integration tests with PHPUnit using mocks and assertions. Practice test-driven development on your domain logic.
php.challenge: Write a full PHPUnit test suite covering your User class, ReportGenerator, and database layer, targeting at least 80 percent code coverage.

php.title: Modern PHP 8 Features
php.description: Leverage PHP 8 features including named arguments, union types, match expressions, enums, and fibers. Write expressive modern PHP.
php.challenge: Refactor your project to use PHP 8 enums for user roles, match for status mapping, and typed properties throughout all classes.

php.title: Security and Performance
php.description: Harden your PHP application against common vulnerabilities including XSS, CSRF, and injection. Apply caching strategies to improve throughput.

resources.label: Survive the Deep End: PHP Security

php.challenge: Audit your project for OWASP Top 10 issues, add CSRF protection to all forms, hash passwords, and implement Redis caching on expensive queries.

php.title: Production PHP - APIs, Queues, and Deployment
php.description: Build and deploy a production-grade PHP REST API with background job processing and containerized infrastructure. Apply monitoring and zero-downtime deployment strategies.
topics: Building REST APIs in plain PHP or micro-framework
Background queues with database or Redis
php.challenge: Deploy your complete project as a Dockerized PHP-FPM and Nginx stack with a REST API, background job queue, Redis caching, and a CI pipeline that runs your PHPUnit suite on every push.

c.title: C Syntax and Compilation
c.description: Learn C syntax, primitive types, operators, and the compilation pipeline from source to executable. Write and compile your first programs using GCC.
topics: Control flow: if, for, while, switch
Standard I/O with printf and scanf
c.challenge: Write a C program that reads integers from stdin, computes min, max, and average, then prints a formatted summary.

c.title: Pointers and Memory Model
c.description: Understand the C memory model, pointer arithmetic, and the relationship between arrays and pointers. Write functions that operate on data via pointers.
c.challenge: Extend your statistics program to process data through pointer-based functions, passing arrays by pointer and returning results via output parameters.

c.title: Strings and Standard Library
c.description: Work with C strings as char arrays and use the standard library for string manipulation, memory allocation, and sorting. Understand null-termination and buffer safety.
c.challenge: Add string-based input to your project: read a CSV of names and scores, parse with sscanf, dynamically allocate storage, and sort entries by score using qsort.

c.title: Structs, Unions, and Enums
c.description: Group related data using structs, model variant types with unions, and improve readability with enums and typedef. Build linked data structures.
c.challenge: Refactor your data model into structs representing students and score records, link them in a singly-linked list, and implement insert and search operations.

c.title: File I/O and Error Handling
c.description: Read and write files using FILE streams and handle errors robustly using errno and return-code conventions. Implement resource cleanup on all exit paths.
topics: Cleanup with goto or nested ifs
c.challenge: Persist your linked list to a binary file, reload it on startup, and handle all fopen and fread errors with descriptive messages and clean exits.

c.title: Makefiles and Build Systems
c.description: Organize a multi-file C project with header files and a Makefile that supports incremental builds, compiler warnings, and release and debug targets.
topics: Makefile rules, variables, and phony targets
c.challenge: Split your project into modules (io, list, stats) each with its own .c and .h file, and write a Makefile with debug and release targets and automatic dependency tracking.

c.title: Debugging with gdb and Valgrind
c.description: Debug C programs using gdb for step-through inspection and Valgrind for memory error detection. Eliminate leaks, use-after-free, and buffer overruns.
c.challenge: Intentionally introduce a memory bug into your project, then use Valgrind and gdb to locate and fix it, documenting each step.

c.title: Dynamic Data Structures and Algorithms
c.description: Implement common data structures from scratch in C: dynamic arrays, hash tables, and binary trees. Analyze time and space complexity of each.
c.challenge: Replace the linked list in your project with a hash table keyed by student ID, and add a binary search tree for sorted traversal by score.

c.title: Systems Programming - Processes and Sockets
c.description: Use POSIX APIs to spawn processes, handle signals, and communicate over TCP sockets. Understand file descriptors and the Unix process model.
c.challenge: Turn your stats project into a client-server app: a server that accepts TCP connections and returns student statistics, and a client that sends queries.

c.title: Production C - Portability, Profiling, and Embedding
c.description: Write portable, production-quality C using CMake, profiling tools, and sanitizers. Understand embedding C as a scripting host and interfacing with other languages.
topics: Calling C from Python via ctypes
Defensive coding and static analysis with cppcheck
c.challenge: Package your client-server project as a CMake build, profile it with gprof to identify bottlenecks, expose the stats engine as a shared library, and call it from a Python script via ctypes.

kotlin.title: Kotlin Syntax and Basics
kotlin.description: Learn Kotlin syntax, type inference, and basic constructs. Understand how Kotlin differs from Java with its concise and expressive style.
topics: Control flow: if, when, for, while
kotlin.challenge: Write a Kotlin command-line app that reads a list of task strings, filters by a keyword, sorts alphabetically, and prints them with indices.

kotlin.title: Null Safety and Type System
kotlin.description: Master Kotlin null safety with nullable types, the Elvis operator, and safe calls. Understand smart casts and sealed classes for exhaustive type handling.
topics: Nullable types and safe call operator
Smart casts with is and as
kotlin.challenge: Add optional metadata to your task app using nullable types, handle missing fields with Elvis operators, and model task status as a sealed class.

kotlin.title: OOP - Classes, Data Classes, and Objects
kotlin.description: Use Kotlin classes, data classes, object declarations, and delegation to build concise and idiomatic OOP code. Understand primary constructors and init blocks.
kotlin.challenge: Model your task app entities as data classes, use a companion object for factory methods, and implement interface delegation for pluggable storage backends.

kotlin.title: Functional Programming and Collections
kotlin.description: Apply functional programming with Kotlin lambdas, higher-order functions, and the collections API. Use sequences for lazy evaluation of large datasets.
topics: Scope functions: let, run, with, apply, also
kotlin.challenge: Rewrite all data manipulation in your task app using Kotlin collection operations - group tasks by category, compute stats, and chain operations with sequences.

kotlin.title: Extension Functions and DSLs
kotlin.description: Extend existing classes without inheritance using extension functions and properties. Build internal DSLs with lambda receivers and infix functions.
kotlin.challenge: Create a task-builder DSL using lambda receivers so tasks can be constructed with a clean block syntax, and add extension functions on List<Task> for common queries.

kotlin.title: Coroutines and Async Programming
kotlin.description: Write non-blocking async code using Kotlin coroutines with suspend functions, structured concurrency, and Flow for reactive streams. Replace callbacks and threads.
kotlin.challenge: Add async I/O to your task app: load tasks from a simulated remote source using coroutines, expose updates as a Flow, and collect them in a coroutine scope.

kotlin.title: Testing Kotlin Applications
kotlin.description: Test Kotlin code idiomatically using JUnit 5 with Kotlin extensions, MockK for mocking, and Kotest for expressive specs. Test coroutine-based code with runTest.
kotlin.challenge: Write a full test suite for your task app covering domain logic, storage backends (using MockK), and the async Flow pipeline using runTest.

kotlin.title: Kotlin Multiplatform and Stdlib Internals
kotlin.description: Explore the Kotlin standard library deeply and understand Kotlin Multiplatform for sharing code across JVM, native, and JavaScript targets.
kotlin.challenge: Extract the core domain logic of your task app into a KMP shared module and provide JVM and JS platform-specific implementations for storage.

kotlin.title: Kotlin on the JVM - Performance and Interop
kotlin.description: Understand how Kotlin compiles to JVM bytecode and how to interoperate cleanly with Java libraries. Analyze performance characteristics and avoid common pitfalls.
topics: Calling Java from Kotlin and vice versa
kotlin.challenge: Integrate a Java library into your task app, expose Kotlin code to Java consumers using @JvmStatic annotations, and use inline reified functions for type-safe serialization.

kotlin.title: Production Kotlin - Ktor, DI, and Deployment
kotlin.description: Build and deploy a production Kotlin backend using Ktor for HTTP, Koin for dependency injection, and Docker for containerization. Apply structured concurrency throughout.
kotlin.challenge: Deploy your task app as a Ktor REST API with Koin DI, kotlinx.serialization, coroutine-based handlers, a Docker container, and a GitHub Actions CI pipeline.

swift.title: Swift Syntax and Basics
swift.description: Learn Swift syntax, constants, variables, and basic control flow in Xcode Playgrounds. Understand Swift type safety and type inference.
topics: Basic types: Int, Double, String, Bool
Control flow: if, switch, for-in, while
swift.challenge: Write a Swift playground that models a simple inventory: add items, remove them, compute total value, and print a formatted report.

swift.title: Optionals and Error Handling
swift.description: Master Swift optionals with optional binding, guard statements, and nil coalescing. Handle errors with throwing functions and do-catch blocks.
swift.challenge: Add file-based persistence to your inventory app, using throwing functions for I/O errors, guard for validation, and Result for operation outcomes.

swift.title: Structs, Classes, and Value Semantics
swift.description: Understand the distinction between value types (structs, enums) and reference types (classes) in Swift. Apply value semantics for predictable state management.
swift.challenge: Refactor your inventory app to use structs for Item and value-semantic collections, ensuring mutations are explicit and testable.

swift.title: Protocols and Generics
swift.description: Define behavior with protocols and write reusable generic types and functions. Understand protocol-oriented programming as the Swift paradigm.
swift.challenge: Define a generic Repository protocol with associated types for your inventory entities, provide an in-memory and a file-backed conformance.

swift.title: Collections, Functional Patterns, and Closures
swift.description: Use Swift collections idiomatically with map, filter, reduce, and compactMap. Write expressive closures with capture lists and trailing syntax.
swift.challenge: Rewrite all query logic in your inventory app using Swift collection operations, grouping items by category and computing aggregates with reduce.

swift.title: Concurrency - Async/Await and Actors
swift.description: Write safe concurrent code using Swift structured concurrency with async/await, actors, and task groups. Eliminate data races at compile time.
swift.challenge: Convert your repository layer to use async/await for simulated network fetches, protect shared state with an actor, and load items concurrently using TaskGroup.

swift.title: Swift Package Manager and Ecosystem
swift.description: Manage Swift packages with the Swift Package Manager, create reusable libraries, and integrate third-party dependencies. Understand manifest authoring.
swift.challenge: Extract the domain and repository layer of your inventory app into a standalone Swift package and depend on it from the main app target.

swift.title: Testing with XCTest and Swift Testing
swift.description: Test Swift code with XCTest and the new Swift Testing framework using macros. Write async tests, mock protocols, and achieve meaningful coverage.
topics: Testing async code with async tests
swift.challenge: Write a complete test suite for your inventory package using Swift Testing macros, covering repository operations, async fetch, and actor-protected state.

swift.title: Advanced Swift - Macros, Result Builders, and Reflection
swift.description: Use Swift macros and result builders to reduce boilerplate and create expressive APIs. Explore runtime reflection with Mirror for debugging and serialization.
topics: Writing a Swift macro with SwiftSyntax
Custom string convertible and debug descriptions
swift.challenge: Add a result builder to your inventory DSL for constructing item lists declaratively, and write a macro to auto-generate equality and description implementations.

swift.title: Production Swift - Server-Side and Cross-Platform
swift.description: Deploy Swift as a server-side language using Vapor, run it on Linux inside Docker, and apply production patterns including logging, metrics, and CI.
topics: CI with GitHub Actions on Linux runners
swift.challenge: Deploy your inventory app as a Vapor REST API on Linux via Docker, with Fluent persistence, async handlers, structured logging, and a GitHub Actions CI pipeline.

dart.title: Dart Syntax and Basics
dart.description: Learn Dart syntax, types, and control flow using DartPad. Understand Dart as a strongly-typed, compiled language designed for fast development.
topics: Built-in types: int, double, String, bool, List, Map
Control flow: if, for, while, switch
dart.challenge: Write a Dart script that manages a list of contacts (name, email, phone), supports add/remove/search, and prints a formatted directory.

dart.title: Null Safety in Dart
dart.description: Understand Dart sound null safety with nullable and non-nullable types, late initialization, and the required keyword. Eliminate null-related runtime errors.
dart.challenge: Refactor your contact app to use full null safety with nullable optional fields, late initialization for computed properties, and required constructor params.

dart.title: OOP - Classes and Mixins
dart.description: Model your domain with Dart classes, abstract classes, and mixins. Understand Dart single-inheritance with mixin-based composition.
dart.challenge: Refactor contacts into a class hierarchy: a base Entity class, a Contact subclass, and a Searchable mixin, with factory constructors for JSON parsing.

dart.title: Async Programming - Futures and Streams
dart.description: Write async Dart code using Futures, async/await, and Streams for event-driven data. Handle errors in async contexts and transform streams with operators.
dart.challenge: Add async contact loading from a simulated API using Futures, expose a Stream of contact updates via StreamController, and handle errors with catchError.

dart.title: Collections and Functional Patterns
dart.description: Use Dart collections idiomatically with the Iterable API, collection-if, collection-for, and spread operators. Apply functional patterns for data transformation.
dart.challenge: Rewrite your contact query logic using Iterable chaining, collection-if for conditional fields, and spread operators for merging contact lists.

dart.title: pub, Packages, and Project Structure
dart.description: Manage Dart packages with pub, structure a multi-file project, and publish a package to pub.dev. Understand pubspec.yaml, lockfiles, and dependency resolution.
topics: pub get, pub upgrade, pub publish
dart.challenge: Extract your contact domain into a publishable Dart package with a library export, dartdoc comments, example folder, and analysis_options.yaml.

dart.title: Testing Dart Code
dart.description: Test Dart applications with the test package, generate mocks with mockito or mocktail, and measure coverage with dart test --coverage. Write async tests.
dart.challenge: Write a comprehensive test suite for your contact package covering synchronous logic, async stream behavior, and storage operations using mocktail mocks.

dart.title: Dart Isolates and Concurrency
dart.description: Run CPU-intensive work in separate Dart isolates to avoid blocking the event loop. Use SendPort and ReceivePort for message-passing between isolates.
dart.challenge: Offload bulk contact CSV parsing into an isolate using Isolate.run, send results back to the main isolate, and display progress via a Stream.

dart.title: Dart FFI and Native Interop
dart.description: Call native C libraries from Dart using dart:ffi. Understand memory layout, struct mapping, and lifecycle management when crossing the Dart-native boundary.
topics: Memory allocation with calloc and malloc
dart.challenge: Write a Dart FFI binding to a small C library that performs fast text search, integrate it into your contact app for name lookup, and manage native memory safely.

dart.title: Production Dart - CLI Tools, Compilation, and Deployment
dart.description: Compile Dart to native executables and deploy as CLI tools or containerized HTTP servers with shelf. Apply production logging, error handling, and CI.
topics: dart compile exe for native binaries
Building CLI tools with args package
dart.challenge: Ship your contact app as both a native CLI binary compiled with dart compile exe and a shelf REST API in Docker, with a GitHub Actions pipeline building both artifacts.

elixir.title: Elixir Syntax and Basics
elixir.description: Learn Elixir syntax, immutable data types, and the interactive shell iex. Understand the role of atoms and the functional, dynamic nature of Elixir.
topics: Basic types: integers, floats, atoms, strings, lists, tuples
elixir.challenge: Write an Elixir module that processes a list of transactions (amount, category), filters by category, and returns totals using the pipe operator.

elixir.title: Pattern Matching and Control Flow
elixir.description: Master Elixir pattern matching in function heads, case expressions, and with macros. Use guards to add conditional logic to pattern matches.
elixir.challenge: Refactor your transaction processor to use multi-clause functions with pattern matching and guards for categorization logic.

elixir.title: Recursion and the Enum Module
elixir.description: Write recursive algorithms idiomatically with tail-call optimization and use the Enum and Stream modules for declarative data processing.
elixir.challenge: Rewrite all data transformations in your transaction processor using Enum and Stream pipelines, including a lazy stream for reading large CSV files.

elixir.title: Mix and Project Structure
elixir.description: Use Mix to create, build, and test Elixir projects. Manage dependencies from Hex and generate documentation with ExDoc.
topics: mix compile, mix run, mix test
elixir.challenge: Convert your transaction processor into a proper Mix project with Hex dependencies for CSV parsing and ExDoc-generated documentation.

elixir.title: Processes and Message Passing
elixir.description: Work directly with Elixir processes using spawn, send, and receive. Understand the actor model and how process isolation enables fault tolerance.
topics: Spawning processes with spawn and spawn_link
elixir.challenge: Spawn worker processes to handle transaction batches in parallel, collect results through message passing, and aggregate the final report in the coordinator process.

elixir.title: OTP - GenServer, Supervisor, and Agent
elixir.description: Build fault-tolerant systems using OTP behaviours: GenServer for stateful processes, Supervisor for error recovery, and Agent for simple shared state.
elixir.challenge: Wrap your transaction processor in a GenServer that holds state, supervise it with a Supervisor, and implement automatic restart on crash.

elixir.title: Metaprogramming and Macros
elixir.description: Extend Elixir using macros that manipulate the AST at compile time. Use quote and unquote to generate code, and understand when not to use macros.
elixir.challenge: Write a macro that auto-generates validation functions for transaction structs from a declarative spec, reducing boilerplate in your GenServer.

elixir.title: Testing with ExUnit and Property Testing
elixir.description: Test Elixir applications with ExUnit, use Mox for behaviour-based mocking, and apply property-based testing with StreamData to find edge cases.
topics: Mox for defining and verifying mocks
elixir.challenge: Write a full test suite for your OTP transaction processor using ExUnit, Mox for external dependencies, and StreamData properties for transaction validation logic.

elixir.title: Distributed Elixir and ETS
elixir.description: Connect Elixir nodes into a distributed cluster and use ETS for high-performance in-memory storage. Understand the BEAM distribution model.
elixir.challenge: Add an ETS-backed cache to your transaction processor, run two nodes connected together, and demonstrate that one node can query another node process by name.

elixir.title: Production Elixir - Phoenix, Releases, and Deployment
elixir.description: Deploy a production Elixir application using Phoenix for HTTP, mix release for self-contained OTP releases, and Docker for containerized deployment.
topics: Docker and CI with GitHub Actions
elixir.challenge: Wrap your transaction processor in a Phoenix REST API with LiveView dashboard, package it as a mix release in Docker, and deploy it with a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline.

scala.title: Scala Syntax and Basics
scala.description: Learn Scala syntax, type inference, and the REPL. Understand Scala as a hybrid object-functional language on the JVM.
topics: Control flow: if, match, for, while
scala.challenge: Write a Scala script that reads a list of employee records, filters by department, computes salary statistics, and prints a summary.

scala.title: OOP in Scala - Classes, Traits, and Objects
scala.description: Model domains with Scala classes, traits, and case classes. Understand companion objects and how Scala OOP unifies with functional idioms.
scala.challenge: Refactor your employee records into case classes with sealed trait hierarchies for employment type, using companion objects for factory methods.

scala.title: Functional Programming - Collections and Higher-Order Functions
scala.description: Use Scala collections functionally with map, flatMap, filter, and fold. Model optionality with Option and chain operations with for-comprehensions.
topics: Immutable collections: List, Vector, Map, Set
scala.challenge: Rewrite all data processing in your employee app using immutable Scala collections, Option for nullable fields, and for-comprehensions for multi-step queries.

scala.title: Pattern Matching and Algebraic Data Types
scala.description: Model data with algebraic data types using sealed traits and case classes. Use exhaustive pattern matching to process ADTs safely and expressively.
scala.challenge: Model all business events in your app as sealed trait ADTs and write a pattern-matching event processor that handles every case exhaustively.

scala.title: Type Classes and Implicits
scala.description: Implement ad-hoc polymorphism with type classes using Scala 3 given and using clauses. Understand how the compiler resolves implicit parameters.
scala.challenge: Define Show and Eq type classes for your domain models, provide given instances, and use them in a generic report renderer without subtyping.

scala.title: sbt Deep Dive and Project Structure
scala.description: Master sbt for multi-module Scala projects, custom tasks, and plugin integration. Configure build settings, aggregation, and publish workflows.
scala.challenge: Reorganize your project into sbt multi-modules (core, api, cli), configure scalafmt and scoverage, and set up a sbt publish workflow.

scala.title: Concurrency - Futures and Akka
scala.description: Write concurrent Scala code using Futures with ExecutionContext and the Akka actor framework. Handle asynchronous failures and backpressure with Akka Streams.
scala.challenge: Process employee records in parallel using Futures, model stateful aggregation as an Akka actor, and stream large reports with Akka Streams.

scala.title: Cats and Functional Effect Systems
scala.description: Use the Cats library for functional abstractions and Cats Effect IO for purely functional async programming. Model side effects as values.
scala.challenge: Rewrite your data pipeline using Cats Effect IO for all side effects, manage database connections with Resource, and use Fiber for parallel loading.

scala.title: Testing Scala with ScalaTest and ScalaCheck
scala.description: Test Scala code with ScalaTest using multiple spec styles, generate test cases with ScalaCheck property-based testing, and mock dependencies with Mockito.
topics: Testing IO with Cats Effect TestControl
scala.challenge: Write a full test suite for your employee pipeline using ScalaTest WordSpec, ScalaCheck properties for domain validation, and Cats Effect TestControl for IO tests.

scala.title: Production Scala - HTTP4s, Doobie, and Deployment
scala.description: Build and deploy a production Scala service using http4s for HTTP, Doobie for type-safe SQL, and Docker for containerized deployment with CI.
topics: CI/CD with GitHub Actions and GraalVM native image
scala.challenge: Deploy your employee service as an http4s REST API with Doobie persistence, Circe serialization, Cats Effect IO throughout, Docker packaging, and a full GitHub Actions CI pipeline.

haskell.title: Haskell Syntax and GHCi
haskell.description: Learn Haskell syntax, pure functions, and type signatures using GHCi. Understand Haskell as a purely functional, lazy, statically typed language.
topics: Basic types: Int, Integer, Double, Bool, Char
haskell.challenge: Write Haskell functions to process a list of numbers: compute median, mode, and variance, with explicit type signatures for every function.

haskell.title: Lists, Recursion, and Pattern Matching
haskell.description: Work with Haskell lists using recursion and pattern matching on constructors. Understand lazy evaluation and how infinite lists are possible.

resources.label: Learn You a Haskell - Lists Chapter

haskell.challenge: Implement a statistics module using only recursion and pattern matching: mean, median, standard deviation, and a histogram using list comprehensions.

haskell.title: Type Classes and Polymorphism
haskell.description: Define and use type classes for ad-hoc polymorphism. Understand standard type classes Eq, Ord, Show, Read, Enum, and Num.
haskell.challenge: Define a DataPoint type class with methods for summarization, derive Eq and Ord for your stat records, and write polymorphic reporting functions constrained by Show.

haskell.title: Algebraic Data Types and Maybe/Either
haskell.description: Model domains with Haskell algebraic data types using sum and product types. Use Maybe and Either to handle failure without exceptions.
haskell.challenge: Model your statistics domain as Haskell ADTs, use Either for parsing errors, and chain operations with Maybe to handle missing data without exceptions.

haskell.title: Functors, Applicatives, and Monads
haskell.description: Understand the Functor, Applicative, and Monad type classes that unify computation patterns. Use do-notation as syntactic sugar for monadic code.
topics: Maybe, Either, and List as monads
haskell.challenge: Rewrite your parsing and validation pipeline using Applicative for independent validations and Monad do-notation for sequential, error-aware computations.

haskell.title: IO Monad and Side Effects
haskell.description: Perform side effects in Haskell through the IO monad, separating pure and impure code. Use IORef for mutable state when necessary.
topics: Reading and writing files in IO
Exception handling with try and catch
haskell.challenge: Add file I/O to your statistics program: read data from CSV in IO, perform all processing in pure functions, and write results to a report file.

haskell.title: Cabal, Stack, and the Haskell Ecosystem
haskell.description: Manage Haskell projects with Cabal and Stack, add dependencies from Hackage and Stackage, and structure multi-module packages.
haskell.challenge: Convert your stats program into a Cabal package with library and executable targets, add aeson and text as dependencies, and configure GHC warnings.

haskell.title: Advanced Type System - GADTs and Type Families
haskell.description: Use advanced GHC extensions to enforce invariants at the type level with GADTs, type families, and DataKinds. Write type-safe DSLs.
topics: Type families: type and data families
haskell.challenge: Model your statistics pipeline as a type-safe GADT where the type parameter encodes whether the data has been validated, preventing misuse at compile time.

haskell.title: Concurrency and STM
haskell.description: Write concurrent Haskell using lightweight threads, MVar for synchronization, and Software Transactional Memory for composable atomic operations.
haskell.challenge: Process multiple CSV files concurrently using the async library, accumulate results in a TVar via STM transactions, and output a merged statistics report.

haskell.title: Production Haskell - Servant, Persistent, and Deployment
haskell.description: Build and deploy a production Haskell web service with type-safe APIs using Servant, database access with Persistent, and Docker containerization.
haskell.challenge: Deploy your statistics service as a Servant REST API with Persistent database backend, packaged in a multi-stage Docker build, with a GitHub Actions CI pipeline running HSpec tests.

lua.title: Lua Syntax and Basics
lua.description: Learn Lua syntax, dynamic typing, and the interactive REPL. Understand Lua as a lightweight embeddable scripting language.
topics: Basic types: nil, boolean, number, string
Control flow: if, while, repeat, for
lua.challenge: Write a Lua script that reads a list of player names and scores, ranks them, and prints a leaderboard with formatted position, name, and score columns.

lua.title: Tables - The Universal Data Structure
lua.description: Master Lua tables as the single mechanism for arrays, dictionaries, sets, and objects. Understand table constructors and iteration patterns.
topics: Table library: insert, remove, sort, concat
lua.challenge: Extend your leaderboard to support multiple game modes, storing nested tables of scores per mode, sorting each mode independently, and displaying a combined ranking.

lua.title: Functions, Closures, and Iterators
lua.description: Use Lua closures for encapsulation and state, write custom iterators for for-in loops, and handle variadic arguments. Understand upvalues.
lua.challenge: Add a paginated iterator to your leaderboard that yields one page of results at a time, implemented as a closure capturing page state.

lua.title: Metatables and OOP
lua.description: Implement object-oriented programming in Lua using metatables and the __index metamethod. Build class systems with inheritance.
lua.challenge: Implement a Player class and a Leaderboard class using metatables, with inheritance so VerifiedPlayer extends Player and overrides the score display method.

lua.title: Coroutines
lua.description: Use Lua coroutines for cooperative multitasking, generator patterns, and async-style control flow. Understand the difference from OS threads.
topics: Scheduling with a simple event loop
lua.challenge: Implement a coroutine-based game loop that processes player input events one at a time, yielding between each event and simulating async score submission.

lua.title: Modules and Package System
lua.description: Structure Lua programs using the module pattern and the require system. Understand package.path, package.loaded, and creating reusable Lua libraries.
lua.challenge: Split your leaderboard application into modules: leaderboard, player, persistence, and ui, each returning a table of functions, loaded via require.

lua.title: File I/O and the Standard Library
lua.description: Use Lua standard library modules io, os, string, and math for file handling, system interaction, and text processing. Master Lua string patterns.
lua.challenge: Persist the leaderboard to a CSV file using io, parse it back with string.gmatch patterns, and add os.date timestamps to every score entry.

lua.title: Embedding Lua and the C API
lua.description: Embed Lua into a C application using the Lua C API. Push and pop values on the Lua stack, call Lua from C, and expose C functions to Lua scripts.
topics: Lua stack: push, pop, and type checking
lua.challenge: Embed Lua in a C host application: expose a C score-calculation function to Lua scripts, load a leaderboard config script from Lua, and use lua_pcall for safe execution.

lua.title: LuaJIT and Performance Optimization
lua.description: Run Lua at near-native speed using LuaJIT and its tracing JIT compiler. Use LuaJIT FFI to call C functions without the C API overhead.
lua.challenge: Port your score computation to LuaJIT, use FFI to call a C math library function, profile the hot path with jit.p, and compare throughput against standard Lua.

lua.title: Production Lua - OpenResty, Testing, and Deployment
lua.description: Deploy production Lua services using OpenResty for high-performance HTTP, manage packages with LuaRocks, and test with the Busted framework.
lua.challenge: Deploy your leaderboard as an OpenResty HTTP API with shared memory caching, LuaRocks-managed dependencies, a Busted test suite, and a Docker container with nginx config.
